question: Will the highest temperature in Seoul be 18°C or higher on April 17?

fragility_points: Consumer forecast pages may share overlapping model inputs.
A late airport-specific forecast update could move the estimate materially.
key_assumptions: RKSI can run cooler than Seoul proper, so city forecasts should be discounted.
City-level warmth still carries enough signal that 18°C remains slightly more likely than not.
main_logical_chain: Identify the governing source and exact settlement station as RKSI via contract/Wunderground.
Compare city-level warm forecasts with airport-area cooler forecast.
Discount market confidence because station/location basis risk is real.
Land on modest Yes rather than market-level confidence.
payload.main_thesis: Yes is modestly favored, but the market likely overstates confidence because settlement is at cooler Incheon Intl Airport Station rather than central Seoul.
quote_anchors: The market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.
payload.source_quality_view: High confidence in the governing source of truth, moderate confidence in contextual forecasts, and medium-low evidence independence overall.
strongest_disconfirmers: Settlement uses Incheon Intl Airport Station, not downtown Seoul.

payload.timing_relevance: Date-sensitive one-day weather market; at research time Apr 17 had not finalized on the governing Wunderground page, so outcome was unverified rather than false.
unresolved_ambiguities: How much cooler RKSI will be than Seoul proper on this specific day.

payload.what_would_change_view: A direct RKSI-specific forecast or early Apr 17 observations clearly above 18°C would move me toward the market; repeated airport-specific sub-17°C forecasts would move me toward No.
